topic_summary: Forensic analysis of a hot isostatic press (HIP) vessel failure: three-factor causation (transient thermal stresses uncalculated due to 1980s computing limits, susceptible microstructure from improper heat treatment by Japan Steelworks, hydrogen embrittlement from corrosion pitting due to inadequate water treatment and inspection). Critical flaw size analysis: a half-inch crack destroyed a 200-ton vessel. Liability spread across material supplier, operator, water treatment company, and inspection company.
cases_referenced: Hot isostatic press vessel failure (Japan Steelworks high-strength steel, 280 ksi, 200-ton vessel)
French generator rotor forging stress corrosion cracking failure (precedent case for molybdate water treatment)
figures_referenced: 1982 (era of original design, pre-modern FEA computing)
